figures.caption: Fig. 1. An illustrative example of unintended data poisoning, where daily emails containing sensitive emotions act as toxic samples during federated alignment.

Fig. 5. Impact of poisoning and defense efficacy across models. Gray bars show the original model performance: the Base model is inherently unsafe (62.8%), while the Instruct model is aligned (10.8%). Red bars indicate that unintended poisoning causes the Base model to revert to high toxicity (58.7%) and degrades the Instruct model. Blue bars demonstrate that FedDetox effectively suppresses toxicity in both scenarios, achieving safety levels comparable to the ideal benign setting (Green bars).

Fig. 2. Problem Definition: The risk of Unintended Data Poisoning in Federated Alignment. Left: In centralized settings, raw data is aggregated and sanitized via a central Safety Filter before training, ensuring a safe model. Right: In federated settings, raw data remains local and private. “Unaware” clients, possessing a mixture of benign and toxic data like private chat history, perform local tuning without sanitization. This Unintended Poisoning propagates toxic gradients to the server, causing catastrophic Safety Degradation in the global model.

Fig. 6. Ablation Study: Effectiveness of sanitization strategies. Simply discarding toxic data (Orange) yields a significantly higher ASR (25.6%) compared to FedDetox replacement strategy (Blue, 14.0%). This confirms that explicit refusal training is necessary to define safety boundaries.

Fig. 3. Overview of the knowledge distillation pipeline for the lightweight Guardian. We construct a mixed dataset of benign and toxic samples to train the compact student model. The student learns to approximate the safety decision boundaries of the teacher by minimizing a joint loss function that combines the KL divergence of soft logits and the Cross-Entropy loss of hard labels.
Fig. 4. Hazard category distribution of the distilled dataset. The dataset is a composite aggregation from multiple sources to ensuring comprehensive coverage of the MLCommons taxonomy.
